# apim_group (Resource)

Group Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "apim_group" "example" {
  hrid = "example"
  name = "Example"
  members = [
    {
      roles = {
        API         = "OWNER"
        APPLICATION = "USER"
        INTEGRATION = "USER"
      }
      source    = "memory"
      source_id = "api1"
    }
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `hrid` (String) A unique human readable id identifying this resource. Requires replacement if changed.
- `name` (String) Group's name.

### Optional

- `environment_id` (String) environment ID
- `members` (Attributes List) Members of this group with their IDP source and role assignments.
Members that do not already exist in the IDP will be ignored. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--members))
- `notify_members` (Boolean) If true, members will be notified when the group is synced with APIM. Default: true
- `organization_id` (String) organization ID

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Group's uuid.

<a id="nestedatt--members"></a>
### Nested Schema for `members`

Optional:

- `roles` (Map of String) Map of scopes to role name defining what the member can do.
- `source` (String) The identity provider source of the member. Not Null
- `source_id` (String) The member's identifier within the identity provider. Not Null

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = apim_group.example
  id = <<-EOT
    {
      "organization_id": "DEFAULT",
      "environment_id": "DEFAULT",
      "hrid": "example"
    }
  EOT
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import apim_group.group-example '{"organization_id": "DEFAULT", "environment_id": "DEFAULT", "hrid": "group-example"}'
```
